title: Source registry record
description: One citable source in the registry (DATA-01/DATA-02/TRUST-06). A fact references a source by id; licence, rights, jurisdiction and source-type are recorded here once, never on the fact.

id.description: Stable short identifier cited by facts.

publisher.description: Organisation or body responsible for the source.

url.description: Canonical URL or stable reference for the source.

covers.description: Plain-English statement of what this source covers.

updateFrequency.description: How often the source is refreshed (for example Continuous, Annual, As amended).

retrievedDate.description: Date the source was last retrieved or checked.

sourceType.description: Provenance class of the source (TRUST-02): primary, secondary, tertiary or grey literature.

jurisdiction.description: Regulatory jurisdiction the source speaks for (TRUST-06): GB, EU or international.

licence.description: Licence and rights status, so re-use obligations are auditable (DATA-02).

id.description: Licence identifier, for example ODbL-1.0 or OGL-3.0.

url.description: URL of the full licence text.

attributionRequired.description: Whether re-use must attribute the source.

shareAlike.description: Whether the licence imposes a share-alike obligation (for example ODbL).

derivedFrom.description: Optional lineage tag (D-12): the parent source id this source is co-derived from (for example trade reports tracing to one press release), or null for a root. The distinct-lineage gate follows this transitively so co-derived sources count as one lineage, not two.

driver.description: For policy sources, the regulatory driver behind a change. Recorded now (DATA-01); the mandate-vs-incentive publication rule is enforced in Phase 4.

type.description: A mandate compels; an incentive only nudges.
